title: Butter Bean, Mushroom & Walnut Loaf
description: A simpler version of the full roll — same deeply savoury filling, cooked in a loaf tin for easy slicing. Straightforward to prepare and still a proper centrepiece.

ingredients.name: tins butter beans (400g each), drained and rinsed

steps.title: Soak the porcini
steps.content: Place dried porcini in a small bowl and cover with 150ml of just-boiled water. Set aside to soak for at least 20 minutes. Once soaked, drain and reserve the liquid — refrigerate or freeze it for use in a gravy another time. Roughly chop the porcini.

steps.title: Toast the walnuts
steps.content: Toast walnuts in a dry frying pan over medium heat until fragrant and just starting to colour. Set aside.

steps.title: Cook the onion and garlic
steps.content: Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-low heat. Add onion, dried thyme and crushed rosemary and cook gently until the onion is completely soft. Add garlic and cook for a further 2 minutes.

steps.title: Cook down the mushrooms
steps.content: Add chestnut mushrooms to the pan and turn the heat up to medium-high. Cook, stirring occasionally, until all moisture has evaporated and the mushrooms are deeply coloured and almost dry. Add the chopped porcini and tamari. Season well with salt and pepper. Cook for 2 more minutes then set aside to cool slightly.

steps.title: Prepare the tin
steps.content: Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Line a 900g loaf tin with baking parchment and brush with olive oil.

steps.title: Make the filling
steps.content: Tip butter beans into a large bowl and roughly mash — mostly smooth but with some texture remaining. Add the mushroom mixture, toasted walnuts, and beaten eggs. Mix thoroughly. Taste and adjust seasoning. If the mixture feels very wet, stir in a tablespoon of oats.

steps.title: Fill the tin
steps.content: Spoon the mixture into the prepared tin, pressing it down firmly and smoothing the top. A compact, even fill will help it slice cleanly.

steps.title: Roast
steps.content: Roast until the top is firm and well coloured.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.content: Leave in the tin for 10 minutes before turning out — this is important for clean slices. Turn onto a board, remove the parchment, and slice with a sharp serrated knife.

notes.porcini_liquid: Strain through a fine sieve and keep in the fridge for up to 3 days, or freeze. It makes an excellent base for a porcini and red wine miso gravy.
notes.texture: Don't over-mash the beans — some texture makes for a better slice and a more interesting bite alongside the walnut chunks.
notes.getting_ahead: The filling can be made up to a day ahead and kept in the fridge. Spoon into the tin and roast when needed.
